data.description:
Adopt the locked typography semantic taxonomy. Add 8 utility classes to src/index.css (.text-display, .text-title, .text-subtitle, .text-body, .text-body-lead, .text-eyebrow, .text-label, .text-meta), flip the global 'body { text-transform: uppercase }' rule so body defaults to normal-case, and add a Button 'sm' variant if missing. Update DESIGN.md with the new semantic type scale section.

Spec: .lattice/notes/typography-spec-20260607.md (mandatory read).

Why: Audit found 9 distinct text sizes, eyebrow weight drift (font-semibold/font-thin/unlabeled), 7+ pages duplicating a fake-button pattern, and 15 inline-style hero overrides. The codebase has no semantic vocabulary, so every component reinvents the same patterns inconsistently. This task creates the foundation; tasks 2-6 in the migration order depend on it.

Acceptance: 8 utilities defined; body uppercase rule removed; Button has default + sm variants; DESIGN.md typography section reflects the spec; existing pages still build (no visual regressions yet — that's the follow-up tasks).
data.title: Typography foundation — semantic utility classes + body case flip + DESIGN.md spec

data.body:
Review verdict: PASS

Acceptance criteria:
- [✓] All 8 utilities defined in src/index.css under @layer utilities with exact properties matching spec (text-display, text-title, text-subtitle, text-body, text-body-lead, text-eyebrow/label/meta).
- [✓] body selector no longer has text-transform: uppercase (src/index.css:82-86 verified).
- [✓] .display-roman still defined unchanged at src/index.css:142-146.
- [✓] Button default is "px-8 py-5 text-sm font-medium" (src/components/ui/button.tsx:58).
- [✓] Button sm is "px-4 py-2.5 text-xs font-medium" (src/components/ui/button.tsx:59).
- [✓] DESIGN.md "Why no headline-lg / body-md tokens?" subsection GONE.
- [✓] DESIGN.md "Semantic type scale" subsection PRESENT with 4 tables (Display / Body / Chrome / Button) + .display-roman preservation note + typography YAML note.
- [✓] DESIGN.md "Global type rules" paragraph rewritten to reflect body case flip — accurate and well-phrased.

Validation:
- Build: PASS (vite v7.3.1, 3.54s, 2778 modules transformed, no errors).
- Typecheck: PASS (tsc --noEmit clean).
- Tests: 143 passed | 4 failed (147 total). The 4 failures all match the documented pre-existing baseline (footer.test.tsx Jacquard branding, navigation.test.tsx mobile abbreviated labels, neighborhood.test.tsx min-h-screen) — same files, same selectors. No new regressions introduced by this PR.
- DESIGN.md lint: 0 errors, 27 warnings (all pre-existing house token reference warnings unrelated to FRAC-51), 1 info.

Findings:
- CSS structure is clean: 8 utilities live inside @layer utilities, after .display-roman, with a clear comment block explaining the relationship. Syntax is valid.
- Cascade reasoning checks out: .text-display sets font-style: normal and overrides the global .font-serif italic — both are class selectors (same specificity), but .text-display is in the utilities layer (declared after base), so it wins. Build success confirms no Tailwind layer ordering issue.
- Button cva base contributes font-mono / uppercase / tracking-widest to both default and sm sizes — matches the DESIGN.md Button table exactly. Other variants (lg, icon, link, ghost, outline) untouched.
- PRD alignment: PRD line 84 still names Space Grotesk / Space Mono / Instrument Serif. That drift is pre-existing (the live stack has long been Inter / JetBrains Mono / Fraunces / Jacquard per DESIGN.md L192-197) and is explicitly acknowledged as not introduced by FRAC-51. Nothing in this PR contradicts the PRDs core direction.
- Body case flip implications: Footer.tsx line 19 "If youre in NYC..." prose will flip from inherited uppercase to mixed-case — this is the deliberate intent of FRAC-51, not a regression. All chrome that needs to remain uppercase (Navbar, Footer NYC label, Home Golden Age Protocol prose, Button) already declares uppercase explicitly via class or via font-serif/font-mono. No egregious CTA flips spotted. Migration of body callsites to the new .text-body / .text-body-lead utilities is correctly scoped to FRAC-52..56.
- .display-roman is genuinely untouched; it only appears in the diff inside the new explanatory comment block.

Recommendation: merge.
